common.copyFailed: Copy failed. Please copy the command manually.

home.title: Build reviewable AI workflows for Claude Code, Codex, and Antigravity.
home.description: Turn loose prompts into requirements, plans, and execution paths your team can inspect, reuse, and verify — without improvising in chat.
home.proofStepOne: Capture what needs to be built
home.proofStepThree: Approve the plan before coding starts
home.proofStepFour: Execute with one agent, one spec
home.installSectionTitle: Pick your tool. Copy the command. Done.
home.installSectionBody: Switch between Claude Code, Codex, and Google Antigravity. One command drops the full workflow into your repo — no backend, no account required.
home.nodeMapAlt: Animated workflow node map showing requirement intake, epic routing, plan review, and execution.
home.terminalHeadline: Launch with one command, then tailor the workflow to your tool.
home.terminalBody: Pick your tool, copy the command, and get the full workflow installed in under a minute. Works in existing repos with no cloud dependencies.
home.terminalTargetsBody: This preview uses the Codex variant, which seeds the repository with workflow folders and guidance files instead of leaving setup hidden in markdown.
home.highlightsTitle: Less AI drift. More reviewable, repeatable delivery.
home.highlightsBody: Give your AI runs a clear boundary — requirements before planning, planning before code, verification before ship.
home.highlightOneBody: Model-aware install paths and shared workflow assets keep Claude Code, Codex, and Google Antigravity in one coherent delivery flow.
home.highlightTwoBody: Requirement, epic, and feature-plan artifacts give AI runs a boundary, measurable acceptance criteria, and a reviewable execution path.
home.highlightThreeTitle: No cloud. No subscription. Just files.
home.highlightThreeBody: Everything installs directly into your repository. No hosted backend, no API keys, no lock-in. Works wherever your AI tool runs.
home.routesTitle: Everything you need to get started.
home.routesBody: Install in one command, inspect the workflow, or browse reusable skills — all before you commit.
home.routeInstallBody: Choose an AI tool, update the command instantly, and copy with clear visual feedback.
home.routeWorkflowBody: See how intake, plan review, verification, and sync connect inside the workflow.
home.routeSkillsBody: Browse the reusable skills that shape planning, execution, and validation across tools.
home.whatYouGetTitle: Everything installs in under a minute.
home.whatYouGetBody: One command drops structured workflow assets into your repository. No hosted service, no account required.
home.whatYouGetItem1: Workflow templates for requirement, epic, feature, and execution phases
home.whatYouGetItem2: Install paths for Claude Code, Codex, and Google Antigravity
home.whatYouGetItem3: 16 reusable skills for planning, execution, and quality checks
home.whatYouGetItem4: No backend, no API keys, no signup needed
home.whatYouGetItem5: Works in new and existing repositories
home.forTitle: Built for developers who ship with AI.
home.forPersonaOneBody: Keep your AI runs focused. Route prompts through requirements and plans before writing a single line of code.
home.forPersonaTwoBody: Give your team a reviewable planning layer. Plans get approved before implementation starts — no more improvising.
home.forPersonaThreeBody: Standardize how AI-assisted work gets captured, planned, and verified. Everyone works from the same structured flow.
home.faqOne: Does it require a cloud service?
home.faqOneAnswer: No. Everything runs locally inside your repository. No hosted backend, no API keys, and no account required.
home.faqTwoAnswer: Claude Code, Codex, and Google Antigravity. Each gets its own install path and command variant.
home.faqThreeAnswer: It adds workflow folders and template files — requirements, epics, feature plans, and execution guides — directly into your repo.
home.faqFour: Does it work in existing repos?
home.faqFourAnswer: Yes. The install command writes files into your project without disrupting your existing structure.
home.faqFive: Can I use only part of the workflow?
home.faqFiveAnswer: Yes. Each phase is independent. Use only the planning templates, only the skills, or the full end-to-end flow.
home.faqSix: Is it for teams or solo developers?
home.faqSixAnswer: Both. Solo developers use it to reduce AI drift. Teams use it to standardize planning and keep execution reviewable.
home.finalCtaTitle: Install the workflow in one command.
home.finalCtaBody: No signup. No backend. Just copy, run, and start building with a structured AI workflow in your repo.

install.title: Pick your tool, then copy the install command.
install.description: This first slice wires the real command variants into a polished mock terminal so the later page can focus on production details.
install.panelBody: The selected command matches the current CLI tool flag from the repository README.
install.targetsBody: Each tool writes workflow files into its native project structure.

workflow.title: See what the workflow actually does before you use it.
workflow.description: The page is split into four beginner-friendly phases. In each phase, you can compare the lightweight path, the command-driven path, and the fully orchestrated path side by side.
workflow.heroBody: Start at the top rail, click one phase, then read downward. Each card shows a real use case, what the workflow gives you, and how the flow moves step by step.
workflow.whyFourPhasesBody: Because a new user usually needs just four decisions: understand the request, shape the plan, do the work, then review and test before shipping. The last node merges review and testing so the top rail stays easy to scan, while the cards below still show the deeper tools used in that phase.
workflow.sectionBody: Each card answers four practical questions for the active phase: when should you use this mode, what real situation fits it, how does the workflow run, and what do you get at the end?
workflow.repoBadge: Static data synced from audited repo sources
workflow.repoSourcesBody: The active phase aggregates the artifacts surfaced across Basic, Advanced, and Power so the visual story still points back to real files in the repository.
workflow.hint: Pick a phase first. Then compare how Basic, Advanced, and Power solve that same moment in the workflow.
workflow.fieldStepHint: Tip: click another node in the mini graph to inspect the next step in this workflow.
workflow.journeyTitle: From idea to shipped feature — one clear sequence.
workflow.journeyStep1Body: Maybe a feature, maybe a fix. The workflow asks what exactly needs to be built before anything else starts.
workflow.journeyStep2Body: A reviewable plan is written before code starts. Scope is locked. The AI has a map to follow.
workflow.journeyStep3Title: Code is written with a plan
workflow.journeyStep3Body: The plan guides execution step by step. Every change has a clear scope. The AI stays on track.
workflow.journeyStep4Body: Review, test, sync — then ship knowing the work is solid and the loop is closed.
workflow.tierBasicHint: Showing the simplest path first — expand to compare all three levels.
workflow.stepClickHint: Click a node to inspect that step

skills.title: Browse the skills before opening the repo tree.
skills.description: The catalog is driven by static TypeScript metadata now and will be tightened in later slices.
skills.emptyTitle: No skills for this tool yet.
skills.emptyBody: Try another compatibility filter or return to all skills.

footer.title: > ai-workflow
footer.body: Open-source workflow assets for developers who want clearer AI execution paths.

notFound.title: Skill not found.
notFound.description: The requested skill route does not exist in the current static catalog.
